\doxysubsection{Detailed Description}
A collection of useful operations to perform on s-\/expressions. 

A set of routines for operating on s-\/expressions.

\doxysubsection{Function Documentation}
\Hypertarget{sexp__ops_8h_aee78f8a169907a4be034c9e8a696adde}\index{sexp\_ops.h@{sexp\_ops.h}!bfs\_find\_sexp@{bfs\_find\_sexp}}
\index{bfs\_find\_sexp@{bfs\_find\_sexp}!sexp\_ops.h@{sexp\_ops.h}}
\doxysubsubsection{\texorpdfstring{bfs\_find\_sexp()}{bfs\_find\_sexp()}}
{\footnotesize\ttfamily \label{sexp__ops_8h_aee78f8a169907a4be034c9e8a696adde} 
\mbox{\hyperlink{sexp_8h_ae49130a807f3037c4b586b34006adcf2}{sexp\+\_\+t}} \texorpdfstring{$\ast$}{*} bfs\+\_\+find\+\_\+sexp (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}\item[{const char \texorpdfstring{$\ast$}{*}}]{name}{, }\item[{\mbox{\hyperlink{sexp_8h_ae49130a807f3037c4b586b34006adcf2}{sexp\+\_\+t}} \texorpdfstring{$\ast$}{*}}]{start}{}\end{DoxyParamCaption})}

Breadth first search for s-\/expressions. Depth first search will find the first occurrence of a string in an s-\/expression by basically finding the earliest occurrence in the string representation of the expression itself. Breadth first search will find the first occurrence of a string in relation to the structure of the expression itself (IE\+: the instance with the lowest depth will be found).


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}
{\em name} & Value to search for. \\
\hline
{\em start} & Root element of the s-\/expression to search from. \\
\hline
\end{DoxyParams}
\begin{DoxyReturn}{Returns}
If the value is found, return a pointer to the first occurrence in a breadth-\/first traversal. NULL if not found. 
\end{DoxyReturn}
\Hypertarget{sexp__ops_8h_a1d8b75ea0651c3467b7f353d87e3d1ec}\index{sexp\_ops.h@{sexp\_ops.h}!copy\_sexp@{copy\_sexp}}
\index{copy\_sexp@{copy\_sexp}!sexp\_ops.h@{sexp\_ops.h}}
\doxysubsubsection{\texorpdfstring{copy\_sexp()}{copy\_sexp()}}
{\footnotesize\ttfamily \label{sexp__ops_8h_a1d8b75ea0651c3467b7f353d87e3d1ec} 
\mbox{\hyperlink{sexp_8h_ae49130a807f3037c4b586b34006adcf2}{sexp\+\_\+t}} \texorpdfstring{$\ast$}{*} copy\+\_\+sexp (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}\item[{const \mbox{\hyperlink{sexp_8h_ae49130a807f3037c4b586b34006adcf2}{sexp\+\_\+t}} \texorpdfstring{$\ast$}{*}}]{sx}{}\end{DoxyParamCaption})}

Copy an s-\/expression. This is a deep copy -\/ so the resulting s-\/expression shares no pointers with the original. The new one can be changed without damaging the contents of the original.


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}
{\em sx} & S-\/expression to copy. \\
\hline
\end{DoxyParams}
\begin{DoxyReturn}{Returns}
A pointer to a copy of sx. This is a deep copy, so no memory is shared between the original and the returned copy. 
\end{DoxyReturn}
\Hypertarget{sexp__ops_8h_a29ed1d2ac49059377efbfc3aa5d64baf}\index{sexp\_ops.h@{sexp\_ops.h}!find\_sexp@{find\_sexp}}
\index{find\_sexp@{find\_sexp}!sexp\_ops.h@{sexp\_ops.h}}
\doxysubsubsection{\texorpdfstring{find\_sexp()}{find\_sexp()}}
{\footnotesize\ttfamily \label{sexp__ops_8h_a29ed1d2ac49059377efbfc3aa5d64baf} 
\mbox{\hyperlink{sexp_8h_ae49130a807f3037c4b586b34006adcf2}{sexp\+\_\+t}} \texorpdfstring{$\ast$}{*} find\+\_\+sexp (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}\item[{const char \texorpdfstring{$\ast$}{*}}]{name}{, }\item[{\mbox{\hyperlink{sexp_8h_ae49130a807f3037c4b586b34006adcf2}{sexp\+\_\+t}} \texorpdfstring{$\ast$}{*}}]{start}{}\end{DoxyParamCaption})}

Find an atom in a sexpression data structure and return a pointer to it. Return NULL if the string doesn\textquotesingle{}t occur anywhere as an atom. This is a depth-\/first search algorithm.


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}
{\em name} & Value to search for. \\
\hline
{\em start} & Root element of the s-\/expression to search from. \\
\hline
\end{DoxyParams}
\begin{DoxyReturn}{Returns}
If the value is found, return a pointer to the first occurrence in a depth-\/first traversal. NULL if not found. 
\end{DoxyReturn}
\Hypertarget{sexp__ops_8h_a7c51a5bcc075a6cef85de6438085bdb4}\index{sexp\_ops.h@{sexp\_ops.h}!sexp\_list\_length@{sexp\_list\_length}}
\index{sexp\_list\_length@{sexp\_list\_length}!sexp\_ops.h@{sexp\_ops.h}}
\doxysubsubsection{\texorpdfstring{sexp\_list\_length()}{sexp\_list\_length()}}
{\footnotesize\ttfamily \label{sexp__ops_8h_a7c51a5bcc075a6cef85de6438085bdb4} 
int sexp\+\_\+list\+\_\+length (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}\item[{const \mbox{\hyperlink{sexp_8h_ae49130a807f3037c4b586b34006adcf2}{sexp\+\_\+t}} \texorpdfstring{$\ast$}{*}}]{sx}{}\end{DoxyParamCaption})}

Given an s-\/expression, determine the length of the list that it encodes. A null expression has length 0. An atom has length 1. A list has length equal to the number of \doxylink{sexp_8h_ae49130a807f3037c4b586b34006adcf2}{sexp\+\_\+t} elements from the list head to the end of the -\/\texorpdfstring{$>$}{>}next linked list from that point.


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}
{\em sx} & S-\/expression input. \\
\hline
\end{DoxyParams}
\begin{DoxyReturn}{Returns}
Number of \doxylink{sexp_8h_ae49130a807f3037c4b586b34006adcf2}{sexp\+\_\+t} elements at the same level as sx, 0 for NULL, 1 for an atom. 
\end{DoxyReturn}
